title: For an Ordinary Day
scripture_text: This is the day that the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
reflection: The miracle is not the spectacular day. The miracle is the ordinary one. The fact that you are in it, and that it is enough.
body:
Nothing is happening today. There is no crisis. There is no celebration. There is the morning, the lunch, the afternoon, the evening. The traffic, the errands, the long pauses.

This is the day the Lord has made. Not the great days. Not the terrible ones. This one. The ordinary one. The one that will be over by bedtime and never, ever come back.

Notice it. Notice the cup of tea, the way the light comes in, the friend who calls, the dog who is happy to see you. Notice what is not on fire. Notice what is working. Notice that you are alive, and that this is a day, and that the day is a gift.

The Lord is in the ordinary. The Lord is in this.
prayer: Lord, thank You for this day. For the ordinary. For the in-between. Let me see Your hand in it. Amen.
